spec.description: Client-side performance telemetry (channel boxel:client-perf) re-emitted by realm-server as structured JSON log lines and read from Loki. Overview headline signals plus a collapsible row per event type; the User/Session variables drill the whole board into a single client.

panels.description: Distinct client sessions that emitted any boxel:client-perf event in the selected time range. Drill filters (user/session) narrow this.

targets.expr: count(count by (session_id) (count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__range])))

panels.title: Active sessions

panels.description: Count of card-load events in the selected range.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__range]))

panels.title: Card loads (range)

panels.description: Count of wedge events (main-thread stalls) in the selected range. Non-zero is worth investigating in the wedge row below.  A wedge is a main-thread freeze of 10s or more (the 100ms heartbeat missed a tick by ≥10s). Sub-second jank is not counted here; see a keepalive's max_gap_ms for that.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="wedge" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__range]))

panels.title: Wedges ≥10s (range)

panels.description: Count of rebuild events (loader/module refetch cycles) in the selected range.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="rebuild" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__range]))

panels.title: Rebuilds (range)

panels.description: p95 card-to-interactive latency (card-load settle_ms) alongside p95 server-request duration_ms. The headline responsiveness signals.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap settle_ms [$__interval]) by ()

quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: Interactivity p95 (ms)

panels.description: Rate of the signal-gated event types that flag anomalies. wedge and rebuild spikes correlate with degraded client sessions.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="wedge" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))
sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="rebuild" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))

sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="realm-event" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))

sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))

panels.title: Signal event rate (events / interval)

panels.description: Card-to-interactive time (settle_ms): p50/p95/p99 across all card-load events in each interval.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.50, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap settle_ms [$__interval]) by ()

quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap settle_ms [$__interval]) by ()

quantile_over_time(0.99, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap settle_ms [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: card-load settle_ms percentiles

panels.description: Network/loading time (loading_ms): p50/p95/p99 across all card-load events in each interval.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.50, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap loading_ms [$__interval]) by ()

quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap loading_ms [$__interval]) by ()

quantile_over_time(0.99, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap loading_ms [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: card-load loading_ms percentiles

panels.description: Top 20 card_id values by worst observed settle_ms over the selected range. Instant query; widen the time range to survey more history.

targets.expr: topk(20, max by (card_id) (max_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap settle_ms [$__range])))

panels.title: Slowest card loads by card_id (max settle_ms)

panels.description: Card-load event rate per interval, plus the average num_loads (link/dependency loads) per card-load event.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))

avg_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="card-load" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap num_loads [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: card-load rate & avg num_loads

panels.description: p95 duration_ms grouped by endpoint. Isolates which realm-server endpoints dominate client-observed request latency.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__interval]) by (endpoint)

panels.title: server-request duration p95 by endpoint

panels.description: p50/p95/p99 of duration_ms across all server-request events in each interval.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.50, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__interval]) by ()

quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__interval]) by ()

quantile_over_time(0.99, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: server-request duration percentiles (all endpoints)

panels.description: Rate of 4xx/5xx server-request responses grouped by status code.

targets.expr: sum by (status) (count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | status=~"[45].." | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))

panels.title: server-request error rate by status

panels.description: Per-endpoint worst-case duration_ms (query A) and retried-request count (query B) over the selected range. Instant queries merged on endpoint.

targets.expr: topk(15, max by (endpoint) (max_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__range])))

sum by (endpoint) (count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | retried="true" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__range]))

panels.title: Slowest & most-retried endpoints

panels.description: Raw server-request telemetry lines, newest first. Each carries correlation_id for cross-referencing realm-server request logs.

targets.expr: {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="server-request" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}"

panels.title: server-request log lines (correlation_id)

panels.description: p95 deserialize duration_ms grouped by card_type. Surfaces card types whose document deserialization is expensive.

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="deserialize" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__interval]) by (card_type)

panels.title: deserialize duration p95 by card_type

panels.description: Per card_type: avg duration_ms (A), avg doc_bytes (B), avg included_count (C) over the range. Intended as a cost table to spot large-document / high-fan-in card types (a scatter of duration vs size).

targets.expr: avg by (card_type) (avg_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="deserialize" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__range]))

avg by (card_type) (avg_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="deserialize" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap doc_bytes [$__range]))

avg by (card_type) (avg_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="deserialize" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap included_count [$__range]))

panels.title: deserialize cost by card_type (duration vs bytes vs included)

panels.description: Wedge event rate per interval (left axis) and the worst blocked_ms observed per interval (right axis, ms). A wedge is a main-thread stall; blocked_ms is how long the thread was blocked.  A wedge is a main-thread freeze of 10s or more (the 100ms heartbeat missed a tick by ≥10s). Sub-second jank is not counted here; see a keepalive's max_gap_ms for that.

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="wedge" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))

max_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="wedge" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap blocked_ms [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: wedge rate & max blocked_ms

panels.description: Raw wedge telemetry lines, newest first. Expand a line to inspect the nested loaf_scripts (long-animation-frame script attribution) and profiler_stacks payloads.  A wedge is a main-thread freeze of 10s or more (the 100ms heartbeat missed a tick by ≥10s). Sub-second jank is not counted here; see a keepalive's max_gap_ms for that.

targets.expr: {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="wedge" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}"

panels.title: wedge detail (loaf_scripts + profiler_stacks)

panels.description: Which script frame appears most as the main blocking frame across wedges. top_frame_* is the worst LoAF script per wedge; empty frames (longtask fallback) are excluded.

targets.expr: topk(15, sum by (top_frame_function, top_frame_url) (count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | event_type="wedge" | top_frame_function!="" [$__range])))

panels.title: Top wedging frames (by blocking script)

panels.description: Each wedge with its worst blocking scripts (fn @ url:char). The char offset resolves to a line via source map even for minified builds; expand a line for the full loaf_scripts / profiler_stacks.  A wedge is a main-thread freeze of 10s or more (the 100ms heartbeat missed a tick by ≥10s). Sub-second jank is not counted here; see a keepalive's max_gap_ms for that.

targets.expr: {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | event_type="wedge" | line_format "{{.matrix_user_id}}  gap={{.worst_gap_ms}}ms blocked={{.blocked_ms}}ms  {{.top_frames}}"

panels.title: Recent wedges (blocking scripts)
wedge (≥10s main-thread freeze)
panels.description: A wedge is a main-thread freeze of 10s or more (the 100ms heartbeat missed a tick by ≥10s). Sub-second jank is not counted here; see a keepalive's max_gap_ms for that.
Rebuild event rate per interval (left axis) and p95 rebuild duration_ms (right axis, ms).

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="rebuild" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))

quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="rebuild" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap duration_ms [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: rebuild rate & p95 duration_ms

panels.description: Per trigger_modules group: rebuild count (A), avg modules_refetched (B), avg cards_reloaded (C) over the range. Identifies which module changes drive the most expensive client rebuilds.

targets.expr: sum by (trigger_module) (count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="rebuild" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__range]))

avg by (trigger_module) (avg_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="rebuild" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap modules_refetched [$__range]))

avg by (trigger_module) (avg_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="rebuild" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap cards_reloaded [$__range]))

panels.title: rebuild cost by trigger_modules

panels.description: Sum of invalidations_count and reloads_triggered per interval, from realm-event telemetry (index updates observed by the client).

targets.expr: sum(sum_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="realm-event" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap invalidations_count [$__interval]))

sum(sum_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="realm-event" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap reloads_triggered [$__interval]))

panels.title: realm-event invalidations & reloads

panels.description: Realm-event rate split by own_write: true = the client's own write echoed back, false = a change from elsewhere. External churn is the more interesting signal.

targets.expr: sum by (own_write) (count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="realm-event" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" [$__interval]))

panels.title: realm-event own_write vs external

panels.description: p50/p95 of processing_ms (client-side handling time per realm-event).

targets.expr: quantile_over_time(0.50, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="realm-event" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap processing_ms [$__interval]) by ()

quantile_over_time(0.95, {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="realm-event" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap processing_ms [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: realm-event processing_ms percentiles

panels.description: keepalive heartbeats: window_ms (beacon window length) and the max_gap_ms observed within it. Large gaps indicate the tab was backgrounded or the main thread was starved between flushes.

targets.expr: max_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="keepalive" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap max_gap_ms [$__interval]) by ()

avg_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type="keepalive" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | unwrap window_ms [$__interval]) by ()

panels.title: keepalive window & max_gap_ms

panels.description: Every boxel:client-perf event except keepalive, newest first. These signal-gated flushes are the anomaly feed — card-load / server-request / deserialize / wedge / rebuild / realm-event lines as they arrive. Apply the user/session drill filters to focus one client.

targets.expr: {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | event_type!="keepalive" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}"

panels.title: Signal feed (non-keepalive events, newest first)

panels.description: Raw boxel:client-perf feed filtered to the chosen user/session, newest first — the full client timeline including keepalives. Set the session_id textbox (and optionally the user) above to drill into a single client.

targets.expr: {service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}"

panels.title: Per-session timeline (all events for the selected session)
By user — who is hitting issues

panels.description: Users ranked by number of main-thread wedges over the range.  A wedge is a main-thread freeze of 10s or more (the 100ms heartbeat missed a tick by ≥10s). Sub-second jank is not counted here; see a keepalive's max_gap_ms for that.

targets.expr: topk(25, sum by (matrix_user_id) (count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | event_type="wedge" [$__range])))

panels.title: Wedges by user

panels.description: Users ranked by their slowest observed card settle time.

targets.expr: topk(25, max by (matrix_user_id) (max_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | event_type="card-load" | unwrap settle_ms [$__range])))

panels.title: Worst card-to-interactive by user (max settle_ms)

panels.description: Total non-keepalive telemetry events per user — overall anomaly volume.

targets.expr: topk(25, sum by (matrix_user_id) (count_over_time({service="realm-server", env="$env"} | json | channel="boxel:client-perf" | matrix_user_id=~"$matrix_user_id" | session_id=~"${session_id}" | event_type!="keepalive" [$__range])))

panels.title: Signal events by user

list.definition: SELECT DISTINCT matrix_user_id FROM users ORDER BY matrix_user_id;
list.description: Filter every panel to one client's events. 'All' = no user filter.
list.label: User
list.name: matrix_user_id
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT matrix_user_id FROM users ORDER BY matrix_user_id;

list.description: Free-form session id. Leave empty for no session filter; set it to drill the whole board into a single client session.
list.label: Session
list.name: session_id

spec.title: Client Performance
